shelfmark: UTO Sinai Arabic NF M 66, SPP No. 337

layer_record.label: Sinai Arabic NF M 66, Undertext (Greek majuscule)

note: Very small Alexandrian majuscule with influences of Biblical majuscule (in the letters mu, pi, omega). Critical signs, diacritics, and interlinear corrections were added by a later hand (7th-8th c. CE?)

This text comes from a luxurious manuscript, whose folios measured approximately 300 x 250 mm (Turner group II). The letters are 3 mm high and 2-4 mm wide, the space between the lines is 7-8 mm. The margins are large: the internal margin measures 30 mm, the external margin 50 mm, the upper margin 40 mm (the lower margin is not preserved). The written surface is 175 x 155 mm. At the end of the lines the letters are smaller, in order to respect the line division and the justification of the margins, which is a feature typical of luxury codices

Previously unknown hexameters of mythological content on the childhood of Dionysus. The presence of proparoxytone words at the end of the verses, of spondaic verses, and of consecutive spondees rules out the attribution of this work to a follower of the Nonnus of Panopolis school and of his "modern style." The style is rather archaic, as in the uninterrupted reuse of Homeric formulae at the beginning and end of the verses

cataloguer.message: Description and identification of undertexts as part of SPP (2011-2016). Migrated to SMDL in 2025.

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
